elements.sent-2411-001: Abstract: {For a connected graph $G$, we denote by $L(G)$, $m_{G (\lambda)$, $c(G)$ and $p(G)$ the line graph of $G$, the eigenvalue multiplicity of $\lambda$ in $G$, the cyclomatic number and the number of pendant vertices in $G$, respectively.}}
elements.sent-2411-002: In 2023, Yang et al. \cite{WL LT proved that $m_{L(T) (\lambda)\leq p(T)-1$ for any tree $T$ with $p(T)\geq 3$, and characterized all trees $T$ with $m_{L(T) (\lambda) = p(T)-1$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-003: In 2024, Chang et al. \cite{-1 LG proved that, if $G$ is not a cycle, then $m_{L(G) (\lambda)\leq 2c(G)+p(G)-1$, and characterized all graphs $G$ with $m_{L(G) (-1) = 2c(G)+p(G)-1$.}}}

elements.sent-2411-006: AMS classification: 05C50 Keywords: Eigenvalue multiplicity; Line graph; Cyclomatic number; Pendent vertex.
elements.sent-2411-007: In this paper, all graphs are simple, i.e. they have no loops nor multiple edges.
elements.sent-2411-008: We denote the vertex set and edge set of a graph $G$ as $V(G)$ and $E(G)$, respectively.
elements.sent-2411-009: The adjacency matrix $A(G)$ of a graph $G$ with $n$ vertices is an $n\times n$ matrix where the entry in row $i$ and column $j$ is 1 if there is an edge between vertex $i$ and vertex $j$, and 0 otherwise.
elements.sent-2411-010: The eigenvalues of $A(G)$ are called the eigenvalues of graph $G$, and denote by $m_{G (\lambda)$ the multiplicity of $\lambda$ as an eigenvalue of $A(G)$.}
elements.sent-2411-011: If $\lambda=0$, $m_{G (\lambda)$ is also said to be the nullity of $G$.}
elements.sent-2411-012: The line graph $L(G)$ of a graph $G$ is defined as the graph whose vertices are the edges of $G$, and two vertices in $L(G)$ are adjacent if and only if the corresponding edges in $G$ share exactly one common vertex.
elements.sent-2411-013: Let $u$ and $v$ are two distinct vertices in graph $G$, we use $u \sim v$ to denote that they are adjacent.
elements.sent-2411-014: Let $N_G(u) = \{v| v \sim u, v \in V(G)\}$ denote the set of neighbors of vertex $u$ in graph $G$.
elements.sent-2411-015: The degree of a vertex $u$ in graph $G$ is defined as the number of neighbors of $u$, which is denoted by $d_G(u)$.
elements.sent-2411-016: A vertex $u$ in graph $G$ is called a major vertex if its degree $d_G(u)$ is at least 3.
elements.sent-2411-017: A vertex $u$ in graph $G$ is called a pendant vertex if its degree $d_G(u)$ is equal to 1.
elements.sent-2411-018: The number of pendant vertices in graph $G$ is denoted as $p(G)$.
elements.sent-2411-019: We call $|E(G)|-|V(G)|+1$ the cyclomatic number of connected graph $G$ and denote it by $c(G)$.
elements.sent-2411-020: Recently, some publications concentrated on bounding the multiplicity for any eigenvalue of a graph in terms of its structural parameters.
